root: ^The fallen angels rise from the burning lake, rallying to their commander's voice.
^Across the scorched plain, thousands stir — broken wings unfurling, dimmed glories flickering back to life.
^Satan surveys the host. Among them, faces he knows well: Moloch the fierce, Belial the silver-tongued, Mammon whose eyes even now trace the veins of gold in Hell's bedrock.
^My Lord, the host assembles. But we are exposed here upon this blasted plain. We need shelter — a seat of power.
^Let them come at us again! We need no walls — only swords!
^"We shall build. Not a shelter — a palace to rival Heaven itself."
^"Moloch speaks truly. We should prepare for war, not masonry."
^"Mammon — what riches lie beneath this ground?"

c-0: ^A murmur runs through the host. Mammon steps forward, eyes gleaming.
^The earth here is rich, my Lord. Gold, adamantine, fire-forged gems. I have already found veins deeper than Heaven's vaults.
^Give me your workers and I shall raise such a monument that the Almighty Himself shall look down in envy.
c-1: ^Moloch roars approval, raising a fist of iron.
^But Belial steps forward, shaking his head with elegant disdain.
^War? Against the Omnipotent? With what — our shattered lances and scorched wings? We need strategy, not fury. A stronghold gives us time to think.
^Moloch snarls but falls silent. The logic is undeniable.
c-2: ^Mammon kneels, pressing his palm to the blackened earth. His eyes close. When they open, they burn with avarice.
^More than you can imagine, my Lord. This ground weeps gold. The hills are veined with adamant. Hell's wealth surpasses Heaven's — they merely never looked down to see it.
^Then we shall put it to use.

building: ^The work begins. Under Mammon's direction, thousands of fallen angels mine the hills of Hell.
^Mulciber — once Heaven's architect, who built the towers of the celestial city — now designs something new.
^From the earth they draw gold in rivers. Pillars rise like frozen lightning. Walls of burnished bronze catch the firelight.
^It rises impossibly fast, this palace — as if Hell itself wills it into being.
^The structure is vast. A great hall, domed and pillared, lit by hanging lamps of naphtha that burn without consuming.
^My Lord, the great hall nears completion. What shall crown it — a throne, or a council table? A seat of command, or a place of deliberation?
^"A throne. One voice must command — mine."
^"A council table. We fell together — we shall decide together."
^"Both. A throne at the head of a great table."

c-0: ^Mulciber bows. The throne is carved from a single block of obsidian, inlaid with gold and set with gems that burn like trapped stars.
^Beelzebub watches quietly. Belial whispers something to Moloch, but they say nothing aloud.
^Power must be held, not shared. You learned that lesson in Heaven.
c-1: ^A round table of black marble takes shape at the hall's center. Seats for each of the great lords.
^Beelzebub nods approval. Even Moloch seems pleased — a seat at the table is better than standing in the cold.
^We fell because one voice demanded obedience without question. We shall not repeat that error.
c-2: ^Mulciber smiles — the answer of a true ruler. The throne rises at the head of a long table of dark stone, with seats enough for all the great lords of Hell.

naming: ^At last, the palace stands complete. It dwarfs anything in the mortal world — vaster than Egyptian Thebes, grander than Babylon.
^The fallen angels gather in the great hall, thousands strong, their forms reflected in the polished floors.
^It needs a name, my Lord. Every seat of power must have one.
^"Let the host decide. It is their home as much as mine."

c-0: ^The name rolls through the hall like thunder. Pan-dae-mo-ni-um.
^The fallen angels stamp their feet, beat their wings. The sound shakes the pillars.
^And so it is named. The capital of Hell. A monument to defiance.
c-1: ^A ripple of surprise. Then voices rise — shouting, arguing, proposing. Finally, from somewhere in the crowd, a single voice rings clear:
^The name catches like wildfire. It is taken up by a thousand throats.
^Pandemonium. The word was not yours, but it belongs to all of you. Perhaps that is fitting.

conclusion: ^The great doors of Pandemonium swing open for the first time.
^Firelight pours from within — warm, golden, almost welcoming. The fallen angels file in, taking their places.
^For the first time since the fall, something like order exists in Hell. Something like purpose.
^Satan takes his place. The hall falls silent.
^We have built our house. Now... we must decide what to do with our freedom.
^The first council of Hell is about to begin.
